perturbation.original_text: b. If at any time during the term of this Agr eement Data Recipient or an employee of Data Recipient has a change in job responsibilities such that Data Recipient or an employee of Data Recipient becomes a Market Function Employee, Da ta Recipient agrees to notify WECC within five (5) business days of the change in employment and to ensure that Data Recipient or such an employee of Data Recipient discontinues access ing or using any Market Sensitive Information as identified in the WECC Information Sharing Policy.
perturbation.changed_text: b. If at any time during the term of this Agr eement Data Recipient or an employee of Data Recipient has a change in job responsibilities such that Data Recipient or an employee of Data Recipient becomes a Market Function Employee, Da ta Recipient agrees to notify WECC within five (5) business days of the change in employment.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and to ensure that Data Recipient or such an employee of Data Recipient discontinues access ing or using any Market Sensitive Information as identified in the WECC Information Sharing Policy,' it creates an in-text contradiction with section 3. Section 3 places restriction on the use and access of Market Sensitive Information by Market Function Employees. By removing the need to discontinue access, it contradicts the intent of Section 3 and its provision about the use of such information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Market Sensitive Information a. Data Recipient agrees not to prov ide or disclose any Market Sensitive Information as identified in the WECC Information Sharing Policy to any person who is (1) a Market Function Employee as defined by the FERC Standards of Conduct, or (2) actively and personally engaged in day-to- day sales of electric power or other electric power marketing functions.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that if the Data Recipient becomes a Market Function Employee, they must discontinue access to Market Sensitive Information. The changed text removes this requirement. Section 3 explicitly prohibits Market Function Employees from accessing Market Sensitive Information. Therefore, the changed text contradicts Section 3 by removing the requirement to discontinue access, directly conflicting with the prohibition in Section 3. This is a strong in-text contradiction because the modified text removes an obligation necessary to comply with another section of the document.

perturbation.original_text: b. Upon any termination of this Agreement, Data Recipient shall return to WECC all Non-Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession or destroy all Non-Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession and certify to WECC in writ ing that all Non-Public Information has been returned or destroyed, except as may be ot herwise required by law in which case the confidentiality obligations of this Ag reement shall survive termination.
perturbation.changed_text: b. Upon any termination of this Agreement, Data Recipient shall return to WECC all Non-Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession or destroy all Non-Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession and certify to WECC in writ ing that all Non-Public Information has been returned or destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'except as may be ot herwise required by law in which case the confidentiality obligations of this Ag reement shall survive termination,' an in-text contradiction is introduced. Section 4 addresses 'Compelled Disclosures' as required by law. Removing the exception regarding legal requirements from Section 7b now creates a contradiction, stating that all Non-Public information must be returned or destroyed upon termination, even if the law compels disclosure, in contradiction to Section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Compelled Disclosures a. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary here in, Data Recipient may disclose Non-Public Information to a governmental authority as requ ired by law, provided that to the extent permitted by law: i. Data Recipient notifies WECC as soon as reas onably possible of the required disclosure; ii. Data Recipient does not disclose the Non- Public Information until WECC has had a reasonable opportunity to respond to the required disclosure; and iii. Data Recipient cooperates with WECC as re asonably requested by WECC to protect WECC’s interests in the Non-Public Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 7b included an exception for legally compelled disclosures, ensuring consistency with Section 4, which outlines the process for such disclosures. The changed text removes this exception, creating a direct contradiction. Section 7b now mandates the return or destruction of *all* Non-Public Information upon termination, regardless of legal obligations, conflicting directly with Section 4, which permits disclosure if required by law.

perturbation.original_text: c. This Agreement shall be for the sole benefit of WECC and any owner or submitter of the Non- Public Information. This Agreement shall be fu lly enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: c. This Agreement shall be for the sole benefit of WECC and any owner or submitter of the Non- Public Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'This Agreement shall be fu lly enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement.' from section 8, there is an internal contradiction with section 2a, which states 'Data Recipient agrees to take all necessary prec autions to maintain the confidentiality of the Non-Public Information and to prevent unauthorized access to it.' Without the enforceability clause, it becomes ambiguous what remedies or recourse WECC or any other entity has if this section is breached, thus creating a contradiction between the stated obligation in section 2a and the ability to enforce that obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Treatment of Non-Public Information a. Data Recipient agrees to take all necessary prec autions to maintain the confidentiality of the Non-Public Information and to prevent unauthorized access to it.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a clause explicitly stating that the agreement was fully enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non-Public Information was not treated in accordance with the agreement. The changed text removes this enforceability clause. Section 2a states that the Data Recipient agrees to take all necessary precautions to maintain confidentiality. By removing the enforceability clause, the changed text creates a contradiction because it makes it unclear how the confidentiality obligation in section 2a can be enforced. This weakens the agreement and creates a strong contradiction because it removes an entity's ability to act upon breaches of contract.

perturbation.original_text: b. This Agreement represents the entire underst anding between WECC and Data Recipient related to the Non-Public Information and supersed es all previous communications between WECC and Data Recipient related to this subject. Th is Agreement can only be modified by written agreement executed by WECC and Data Recipient. Data Recipient may not delegate its duties or obligations under this Agreement without pr ior written consent from WECC. Any attempt to do so is void.
perturbation.changed_text: b. This Agreement represents the entire underst anding between WECC and Data Recipient related to the Non-Public Information and supersed es all previous communications between WECC and Data Recipient related to this subject. Data Recipient may not delegate its duties or obligations under this Agreement without pr ior written consent from WECC. Any attempt to do so is void.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'This Agreement can only be modified by written agreement executed by WECC and Data Recipient.' from section 8b, it creates an in-text contradiction with the overall understanding that legal agreements need a formal modification process. Without a specific modification process, it contradicts the standard legal framework and best practices, leading to uncertainty on how amendments can be legitimately made to the agreement. It contradicts the implicit expectation that changes require mutual agreement and formal documentation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Miscellaneous Terms a. This Agreement does not require WECC to disclo se any Non-Public Information. Neither this Agreement nor any disclosure of Non-Public In formation grant Data Recipient any intellectual property rights or licenses to such informatio n. Data Recipient agrees to comply with all applicable United States export laws and regulations. b. This Agreement represents the entire underst anding between WECC and Data Recipient related to the Non-Public Information and supersed es all previous communications between WECC and Data Recipient related to this subject. Data Recipient may not delegate its duties or obligations under this Agreement without pr ior written consent from WECC. Any attempt to do so is void. c. This Agreement shall be for the sole benefit of WECC and any owner or submitter of the Non- Public Information. This Agreement shall be fu lly enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement. d. If any term or provision of this Agreement is he ld by a body of competent jurisdiction to be invalid, void, or unenforceable, then (1) the remaining provis ions of the Agreement shall continue in full force and effect, and (2) in lieu of each such term or provision there shall be added as part of this Agreement a clause or pr ovision as similar in terms as possible that is legal, valid and enforceable.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated the agreement could only be modified by written agreement executed by both parties. The changed text omits this clause, creating a contradiction because the document no longer specifies how modifications can be made. This creates uncertainty and conflicts with the implicit understanding that contracts require a formal modification process.
